perturbation.original_text: Use of Evaluation Materials We will not use any to the Evaluation Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. We and our representatives will keep the Evaluation Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Use of Materials We will not use any of the Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. We and our representatives will keep the Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Materials' to 'Materials' in the first sentence but keeping it as 'Evaluation Materials' at the end of the paragraph creates an in-text contradiction. Now, the use restriction applies to 'Materials,' potentially as newly defined above, but the prohibition on use after a non-consummated transaction still refers to the originally defined 'Evaluation Materials.' This introduces ambiguity about which set of information is subject to which restriction, especially if 'Materials' are interpreted to encompass a broader or narrower scope of information than 'Evaluation Materials'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We will not use any of the Materials for any purpose other than the exclusive purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction. We and our representatives will keep the Materials completely confidential; provided, however, that (i) any of such information may be disclosed to those of our directors, officers, employees, agents, representatives (including attorneys, accountants and financial advisors), lenders and other sources of financing (collectively, "our representatives") who we reasonably determine need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction between us and the Company (it being understood that our representatives shall be informed by us of the confidential nature of such information and shall be directed by us, and shall each agree to treat such information confidentially) and (ii) any other disclosure of such information may only be made if the Company consents in writing prior to any such disclosure. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, in the event that a Possible Transaction is not consummated neither we nor our representatives shall use any of the Evaluation Materials for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Evaluation Materials' consistently. The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Materials' in the first sentence, but retains 'Evaluation Materials' later in the same paragraph. This creates a contradiction because the scope of restrictions is now unclear. The first sentence restricts the use of 'Materials,' while the final sentence restricts the use of 'Evaluation Materials' after a non-consummated transaction. This introduces ambiguity about which set of information is subject to which restriction, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Evaluation Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) we or our representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which we are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise our best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) we shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by us or our representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) we or our representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which we are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise our best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) we shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by us or our representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Evaluation Materials' to 'Materials' in the first sentence, the disclosure obligation shifts to the broader 'Materials.' However, the subsequent sentences detailing the permissible extent of disclosure and the assurance of confidential treatment still refer to 'Evaluation Materials.' This introduces a contradiction: while the obligation to consult and resist applies to disclosing any 'Materials,' the permission to disclose and seek confidential treatment is limited to the 'Evaluation Materials' subset. The contradiction arises from the difference in scope, as any disclosure of information falling under 'Materials' but not 'Evaluation Materials' creates ambiguity about the corresponding rights and liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that we or any of our representatives receive a request or are required (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose all or any part of the Materials, we or our representatives, as the case may be, agree to (i) immediately notify the Company of the existence, terms and circumstances surrounding such a request, (ii) consult with the Company on the advisability of taking legally available steps to resist or narrow such request and (iii) assist the Company in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained or that the Company waives compliance with the provisions hereof, (i) we or our representatives, as the case may be, may disclose to any tribunal only that portion of the Evaluation Materials which we are advised by counsel is legally required to be disclosed, and shall exercise our best efforts to obtain assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Evaluation Materials and (ii) we shall not be liable for such disclosure unless disclosure to any such tribunal was caused by or resulted from a previous disclosure by us or our representatives not permitted by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Materials' in the first sentence. This broadens the scope of the initial disclosure obligation. However, subsequent references to 'Evaluation Materials' in the same section, specifically regarding the permissible extent of disclosure and the assurance of confidential treatment, create a contradiction. While there is an obligation to notify regarding the disclosure of 'Materials,' the permission to disclose is limited to 'Evaluation Materials'. This creates a direct conflict in the same section of the document (Changed Text and Contradicted Text are in the same location), as any disclosure of information falling under 'Materials' but not 'Evaluation Materials' creates ambiguity about the corresponding rights and liabilities. This falls under the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the Company's request, we shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written Evaluation Materials and any other written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the Company's request, we shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'Evaluation Materials and' creates an ambiguity as to what "all written materials" refers to. Previously, the clause clearly mandated the return or destruction of Evaluation Materials along with any other written materials related to the possible transaction. Now, without 'Evaluation Materials,' it's unclear if all documents, even those not directly related to the evaluation, must be returned/destroyed. This is contradictory because it broadens the scope of the materials to be returned without providing a limiting principle, potentially conflicting with the earlier definition of 'Evaluation Materials' or 'Materials'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the Company's request, we shall promptly deliver to the Company or destroy all written materials without retaining, in whole or in part, any copies, extracts or other reproductions (whatever the form or storage medium) of such materials.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifically mentions 'Evaluation Materials' as subject to return or destruction. The changed text omits 'Evaluation Materials', broadening the scope to 'all written materials' without specifying a clear limitation. This creates a contradiction because the original text had a specific scope related to Evaluation Materials, while the changed text potentially encompasses a wider range of documents, thus creating an in-text contradiction. The contradiction arises in that the changed text doesn't provide a limiting principle as to what the 'all written materials' refers to.

perturbation.original_text: Although the Company and JPM have endeavored to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of our investigation, we acknowledge and agree that none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied hereunder, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to us or any other person hereunder resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by us or any of our representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Although the Company and JPM have endeavored to include information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of our investigation, we acknowledge and agree that none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied hereunder, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to us or any other person hereunder resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by us or any of our representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'in the Evaluation Materials' implies that information provided may not necessarily fall under the definition of 'Evaluation Materials'. The subsequent disclaimer about the accuracy of 'Evaluation Materials' now contradicts the initial statement by implicitly suggesting that some information might exist outside of the specified definition and lacks a similar disclaimer. The liability clause is linked to 'Evaluation Materials', making the disclaimer incomplete in covering all information shared.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Although the Company and JPM have endeavored to include information known to them which they believe to be relevant for the purpose of our investigation, we acknowledge and agree that none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents is making any representation or warranty, expressed or implied hereunder, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials, and none of the Company, JPM or any of the Company's other representatives or agents, nor any of their respective officers, directors, employees, representatives, stockholders, owners, affiliates, advisors or agents, will have any liability to us or any other person hereunder resulting from the use of Evaluation Materials by us or any of our representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'in the Evaluation Materials', creating a contradiction because the disclaimer regarding accuracy and liability is now limited to 'Evaluation Materials'. The contradicted text is identical to the original text, which explicitly mentions 'Evaluation Materials', implying a broader scope of information covered. By removing this phrase, the changed text implies information exists outside the scope of 'Evaluation Materials' without a corresponding disclaimer, leading to a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations or constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Legal Remedy We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Legal Remedy We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of the confidentiality obligations in this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the initial clause of the 'Legal Remedy' section to breaches of 'confidentiality obligations', a contradiction is created with the subsequent, broader statement that 'Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement'. The initial clause now implies specific performance and injunctive relief apply only to confidentiality breaches, while the second, unchanged, clause suggests these are non-exclusive remedies for *any* breach. This leads to uncertainty about what legal recourse the Company has for breaches of the agreement beyond confidentiality, since the primary remedies have been specifically tied to confidentiality only.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Legal Remedy We understand and agree that money damages would not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of the confidentiality obligations in this Agreement by us or our representatives and that the Company will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement by us or our representatives but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that specific performance and injunctive relief were remedies for *any* breach of the agreement. The changed text limits these remedies to breaches of *confidentiality obligations*. The subsequent clause stating that these remedies are not exclusive for 'a breach of this Agreement' now contradicts the initial, narrowed scope, because the initial clause now only concerns breaches of confidentiality while the 'not exclusive' clause still refers to *any* breach. Therefore, the change creates a strong contradiction as the remedies and their exclusivity are not consistent throughout the section.
